> ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ **Auto-synced ŌĆö do not hand-edit the copies.**
>
> [`u2giants/shared-db`](https://github.com/u2giants/shared-db) is the **single source of truth**. Its entire contents are mirrored into the **`shared-db/` folder** of every consumer repo (CRM, DAM, PM, Directus) on each push to `main`.
>
> **Reading this inside a consumer repo's `shared-db/` folder?** It's a read-only copy ŌĆö edits here are overwritten on the next sync. Change the canonical repo instead.

---

# AGENTS.md ŌĆö cross-app coordination playbook

This is the operating contract for **every AI session working on any of the four
apps that share one Supabase database** (PM/PIM `poppim-web`, CRM `popcrm-web`,
DAM `popdam-web`, and the `directus` backend). Read it before touching code or
the database. It exists to stop four apps ŌĆö often driven by separate AI sessions
ŌĆö from breaking each other through the one database they all depend on.

## 0. The owner is not a programmer

The repository owner directs the work and judges results, but does **not** review
code, manage branches, or merge pull requests. Therefore:

- **The AI owns all git mechanics.** Branches, commits, pull requests, and merges
  are the AI's job from start to finish. Never leave an open PR for the owner to
  deal with ŌĆö open it *and* merge it within the same piece of work, once it is
  safe (see ┬¦4).
- **The owner reviews behavior, not code.** Their feedback is "the board doesn't
  load," "the dropdown is empty." Translate that into changes yourself.
- **Surface risk in plain English.** Before anything hard to undo (dropping a
  column, applying to production, deleting data), explain the risk in one or two
  plain sentences and ask. Approval for one change does not extend to the next.

## 1. Two workflows ŌĆö choose by where you are working

| Where | Workflow | Why |
|---|---|---|
| **An app repo** (`poppim-web`, `popcrm-web`, `popdam-web`, `directus`) | Commit straight to **`main`. No branches.** Build must pass, then push; CI deploys. | One app, one owner, a deploy you can watch. Branches add ceremony with no safety gain. Fix-forward or revert on `main`. |
| **This repo** (`shared-db`) | **Branch + PR, and the AI merges it** once the ┬¦4 checklist passes. | All four apps read these tables. A bad change breaks everyone at once. The PR is a safety checkpoint and an undo button ŌĆö not paperwork for the owner. |

## 2. Why `shared-db` is the dangerous one

Every app reads and writes the **same tables in the same Supabase project**. A
single schema change here can break an app that a different session built months
ago. The database has no "just this app" ŌĆö it is always shared. That is why the
four rules below are non-negotiable for any database change.

## 3. The four anti-collision rules (shared database)

1. **One schema change in flight at a time.** Before starting database work,
   check whether another change is already in progress (┬¦5). If so, finish or
   land that one first, or coordinate with the owner. Two simultaneous schema
   edits are the number-one cause of a broken shared database.
2. **Preview database first. Production never receives untested schema.** Apply
   every migration to the preview branch (`xjcyeuvzkhtzsheknaiu`), prove it
   works, *then* promote to production (`qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai`).
3. **Additive by default (expand, then contract).** Adding a column or table
   cannot break another app. **Renaming or dropping** one that another app reads
   *will*. Default to additive changes. Only rename/drop after explicit owner
   sign-off and a checked deprecation across all four apps.
4. **New timestamped migration files only.** Each change is a new
   `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_*.sql` file. Never edit a migration that has already been
   applied anywhere ŌĆö that is how two sessions silently clobber each other.

## 4. The `shared-db` merge protocol (the checklist the AI runs)

Merge a `shared-db` PR **only when every item is true**:

1. `scripts/check-sql.sh` passes.
2. `supabase db push --dry-run` against the preview branch is clean (only the
   intended changes, no surprise drops/renames).
3. The migration is applied to the **preview** branch and works there.
4. Every app that depends on the change has been tested against preview and the
   owner has confirmed the behavior is correct.
5. The change is additive, or any removal was explicitly approved.

Then: merge to `main` (this auto-syncs the `shared-db/` folder into all apps) and
promote to **production only in an approved window**. Docs-only PRs (no schema
change) need just items 1 and "it reads correctly" ŌĆö merge them promptly.

## 5. How to tell if a change is already in flight

Before starting database work, run these and read the result:

```bash
gh pr list                      # open shared-db PRs
git branch -a && git ls-remote  # in-progress branches
ls supabase/migrations          # files not yet applied to production
git status --short              # uncommitted migration files in the working tree
```

If anything looks like in-progress database work, **stop and serialize** ŌĆö land
it (or ask the owner) before adding your own schema change.

## 6. When two apps need conflicting database changes

Serialize, do not parallelize. Land one change, let it sync, test it, then start
the next. Where possible, prefer one **additive** change that satisfies both apps
rather than two competing edits. If they genuinely conflict, explain the trade-off
to the owner in plain English and let them choose order.

## 7. Project references

```text
Preview project ref:  xjcyeuvzkhtzsheknaiu   (Supabase branch "shared-db-schema-rehearsal")
Production project ref: qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai
```

Never commit anon keys, service-role keys, database passwords, or `.env` files.

## 8. Where to read more

- App rewrite guides: [`docs/ai-session-instructions/`](docs/ai-session-instructions/README.md)
- Shared branch workflow: [`docs/ai-session-instructions/shared-supabase-branch-workflow.md`](docs/ai-session-instructions/shared-supabase-branch-workflow.md)
- Schema ownership map: [`docs/unified-supabase-schema-map.md`](docs/unified-supabase-schema-map.md)
- Migration risks: [`docs/unified-supabase-migration-gaps.md`](docs/unified-supabase-migration-gaps.md)
- CRM production cutover (migrations promoted, Azure OAuth, auto-provision, data import): [`docs/app-migration-notes/popcrm-web-production-cutover-20260621.md`](docs/app-migration-notes/popcrm-web-production-cutover-20260621.md)

## 9. Hosted-Supabase gotchas (do not relearn these the hard way)

These bit the CRM production cutover (2026-06-21). PM/PIM will hit the same ones.

- **PostgREST schema exposure is control-plane config, NOT SQL.** The
  `alter role authenticator set pgrst.db_schemas = ...` + `notify pgrst,'reload config'`
  statements in `20260621151419_crm_rls_realtime.sql` do **not** take effect on
  hosted Supabase ŌĆö the platform overrides them. To expose non-default schemas
  (`api, crm, pim, core`) you must call the Management API:
  `PATCH https://api.supabase.com/v1/projects/{ref}/postgrest`
  with `{"db_schema":"public,graphql_public,api,crm,pim,core"}`. It is a per-project
  setting; **re-confirm it after any project restore/clone**, and set it on the
  preview branch too. If supabase-js suddenly 404s on `api.*`/`crm.*`, check this first.
- **`service_role` has no rights on non-`public` schemas by default.** Server-side
  scripts/workers using the service-role key get "permission denied for schema core/crm"
  until granted. The grants live in `20260621164759_service_role_grants.sql`
  (usage + ALL on tables/sequences for `app, core, crm, pim, plm, ingest, api`,
  plus default privileges). Re-run/verify after adding new schemas.
- **Supabase Auth has one `site_url`; every app needs explicit redirect allowlist entries.**
  The production project's Auth `site_url` is `https://crm.designflow.app`, so OAuth
  flows for PM/DAM/SG/master-data apps must pass an explicit app-origin `redirectTo`
  and that origin must be in `uri_allow_list`. Keep bare origin, trailing-slash origin,
  and `/**` wildcard entries for `crm`, `crm-dev`, `pm`, `pm-dev`, `pm-ci`, `dam`,
  `sg`, and `master` designflow hosts. If Microsoft SSO from one app lands on CRM,
  check `GET /v1/projects/qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai/config/auth` before changing frontend
  routing.
- **Ingested domains are never customers.** `crm.ingested_domain` is CRM-private
  email triage data only. It must not FK to, promote into, source-ref, join as,
  feed picker lists for, or otherwise associate with `core.customer`. The
  corrective migration `20260629034500_remove_ingested_domain_customer_association.sql`
  removed `api.customer_list`, `crm.promote_ingested_domain(...)`,
  `crm.ingested_domain.promoted_customer_id`, and all `directus/ingested_domains`
  customer source refs after 3,741 polluted refs were found in production.
- **`unique nulls not distinct (external_source, external_id)`** on `crm.*` and
  `core.*` tables means you cannot bulk-insert many rows with both columns NULL ŌĆö
  the second NULL/NULL row collides. Importers must set a real
  `external_source` (e.g. `'directus'`) and a unique `external_id` per row.
- **Cross-schema FKs (e.g. `crm.department.company_id ŌåÆ core.company`) are real and
  enforced**, but PostgREST embed syntax (`select=...,company:company_id(...)`)
  may report "no relationship found" because the schema cache does not auto-detect
  cross-schema FKs. The constraint is still there ŌĆö verify with `pg_constraint`,
  not with a failed embed.

content.text:
This changes things ŌĆö I need to be careful here. The shared-db `AGENTS.md` shows it's the source of truth for a shared Supabase DB across multiple apps, with a strict branchŌåÆPRŌåÆpreview-testŌåÆmerge protocol. Two critical facts:

1. **`GridViewState` is app-owned and is *not* defined by any shared-db SQL migration** ŌĆö like its sibling grid tables (`GridLayout`, etc.), it only appears in shared-db's **documentation** (`unified-supabase-schema-map.md`), not in the `core`/`plm` migration schema. It's created by the app's own Sequelize layer.
2. A raw `ALTER "GridViewState"` SQL migration would target a table shared-db doesn't manage and would fail their `supabase db push --dry-run` gate (which I also can't run ŌĆö it needs their Supabase preview branch).

So the correct, consistent "mirror" for an app-owned table is a **documentation record**, matching how its sibling tables are represented there. Let me find the right doc location.

input.command: cd /c/repos/shared-db; echo "=== docs tree ==="; ls -1 docs; echo "=== gaps doc: grid/app-owned mentions ==="; grep -niE "app-owned|GridViewState|Grid|Sequelize|column_state" docs/unified-supabase-migration-gaps.md 2>/dev/null | head; echo "=== schema-map: full grid row + surrounding legend ==="; grep -nE "column_state|app-owned|Sequelize|GridViewState|GridLayout" docs/unified-supabase-schema-map.md | head
input.description: Find where app-owned grid tables/columns are documented

lastPrompt: mirror it to shared-db

file.content:
# POP CRM contact relationship clear RPC ŌĆö 2026-06-26

## Explicit clear flags for contact relationships

What changed:
`20260626171000_crm_update_contact_clear_relationship_fields.sql` replaces
`api.crm_update_contact` with a superset signature that accepts
`p_clear_company`, `p_clear_crm_department`, `p_clear_contact_type`, and
`p_clear_scope`.

Why:
POP CRM edits canonical `core.contact` rows and CRM relationship attributes on
`core.contact_company` through the same RPC. The previous `coalesce()` contract
made `null` mean "leave unchanged", so the frontend could not intentionally
clear account, department, contact type, or scope values.

Future sessions should:
Keep app changes that edit relationship-owned contact fields on this RPC
contract. When a frontend needs to clear a relationship value, send the matching
`p_clear_*` flag; do not rely on `null` alone. Because `company_id` is required

input.content:
# DesignFlow PLM ŌĆö GridViewState.column_group_state ŌĆö 2026-07-02

## App-owned additive column (recorded for schema-map completeness)

What changed:
The DesignFlow PLM app added a `column_group_state` column (JSONB, nullable) to
the app-owned `GridViewState` table. It stores AG Grid column-group open/closed
state as `[{ groupId, open }]`, persisted per saved view alongside the existing
`column_state` and `filter_model` columns.

This is **not** a shared-schema change and there is **no** shared-db SQL migration
for it. `GridViewState` (like its sibling PLM grid/view tables `GridLayout`,
`GridChildrenLayout`, `GridChildrenLayoutOrder`, `GridAccessLevel`, `GridCellNote`,
`viewlayout`) is app-owned and managed by the PLM backend's own Sequelize layer,
not by the canonical `core`/`plm` migrations here. The column is created by:

- Model field: `designflow-backend/models/db/GridViewState.js`
- Idempotent startup migration: `designflow-backend/models/db.js`
  (`ALTER TABLE "GridViewState" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS column_group_state JSONB`)
- Regression assertions: `designflow-backend/tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`

Why:
The RFQ grid now groups its 28 pricing columns into 8 collapsible AG Grid column
groups (Gen/Lic ├Ś FOB/POE/Whse/mDDP). AG Grid keeps column-group open/closed state
in a structure separate from column state, so persisting each user's collapse
choice in their saved view required this additive column. Per the workspace
`SUPABASE-MIGRATION.md` Decision Principle, changes to existing DesignFlow
functionality on an app-owned table stay in the backend service as an idempotent,
additive, non-destructive startup migration ŌĆö which this is.

Why it's recorded here:
`docs/unified-supabase-migration-gaps.md` lists "Generate column-level inventories
for PopDAM tables and PLM Sequelize models" as pending. Until that inventory
exists, app-owned column additions like this one are noted here so the canonical
schema record stays complete. See the PLM grid/view/admin row in
`docs/unified-supabase-schema-map.md`.

Future sessions should:
Keep this column app-owned. If a future phase brings PLM Sequelize models under
canonical shared-db migrations, fold `GridViewState.column_group_state` into that
inventory rather than authoring a standalone `ALTER` migration against a table the
canonical `core`/`plm` schema does not otherwise manage.

content.text:
Done up to the merge ŌĆö and the merge was **blocked by a safety guard**, which I'll leave in place rather than work around. Here's where things stand, in plain terms.

## What I did
The "mirror" is recorded in the canonical `u2giants/shared-db` repo as a documentation note: [PR #34](https://github.com/u2giants/shared-db/pull/34).

A quick but important thing I discovered while doing it: shared-db **doesn't manage this table with database code at all**. Your `GridViewState` table (and all its sibling grid tables) are "app-owned" ŌĆö created by the DesignFlow app itself, not by shared-db's migrations. So the honest, consistent mirror isn't a database migration file (that would point at a table shared-db doesn't control and would fail their checks) ŌĆö it's a **written record** in shared-db's docs saying "the PLM app added this column, here's what/why/where." That's exactly how their own docs say to handle app-owned tables until a later phase inventories them. So the central repo now reflects your new column.

## Why it's not fully finished
shared-db's rulebook says the AI should open **and merge** these doc PRs. But when I went to merge, the system **stopped me** with this reason: I'd be merging my *own* PR with no second person reviewing it ŌĆö which defeats the point of having a review step. That's a sensible guardrail, and I won't try to sneak around it.

## Your options (pick one)
1. **You merge it** ŌĆö open [PR #34](https://github.com/u2giants/shared-db/pull/34) and click the green "Merge" button. It's docs-only, so it's safe and reversible. *(Simplest.)*
2. **Authorize me to merge** ŌĆö if you want me to handle it, you'd need to let this action through (run me outside "auto" mode, or add a permission rule). Then I'll merge and confirm the sync.
3. **Leave it as an open PR** for now ŌĆö the record exists; it just isn't merged into `main` yet.

The actual feature (collapsible groups + saved collapse state) is complete and pushed regardless ŌĆö this shared-db note is purely the bookkeeping you asked for. Which option do you want?

content.text:
Into **`main`** ŌĆö in the `u2giants/shared-db` repo. Specifically PR #34 merges `docs/plm-gridviewstate-column-group-state` ŌåÆ `main`.

I want to flag the thing you're probably reacting to: your standing rule is **never merge to `main`** ŌĆö but that rule is for the **DesignFlow app repos** (frontend/backend/bff), where the flow is `sandbox-albert ŌåÆ develop`. `shared-db` is a *different* repo with a *different* workflow, and its own `AGENTS.md` explicitly says:

Merged. Ō£ģ

PR [#34](https://github.com/u2giants/shared-db/pull/34) is now on `u2giants/shared-db` `main` as commit `cc954f7`. Because merging to `main` auto-syncs the `shared-db/` folder into every consumer repo, your new `column_group_state` column is now recorded in the canonical schema repo and will propagate to the app repos' mirrors on their next sync.

That completes everything you asked for:
- **Feature:** collapsible RFQ pricing groups + per-user saved collapse state ŌĆö frontend PR #141, backend PR #58.
- **DB change:** done the sanctioned way (app-owned additive column via the backend's Sequelize startup migration).
- **Mirror:** recorded in the canonical `shared-db` repo and merged to `main`.

Let me save a note to memory so future sessions handle this repo correctly, since its rules differ from the app repos.

input.content:
---
name: shared-db-canonical-repo
description: Canonical shared Supabase DB repo ŌĆö location, distinct main-branch PR workflow, and when DB changes belong there vs the app
metadata:
  type: project
---

`u2giants/shared-db` is the single source of truth for the shared Supabase database, cloned locally at `C:\repos\shared-db`. Its contents are mirrored (read-only) into the `shared-db/` folder of every consumer repo on each push to `main` ŌĆö never hand-edit those mirror copies.

**Workflow differs from the DesignFlow app repos.** App repos (frontend/backend/bff) use `sandbox-albert ŌåÆ develop`, never `main`. shared-db has **no `develop`**: work on a feature branch, open a PR to **`main`**, and the AI is expected to merge it (docs-only PRs merge promptly after `scripts/check-sql.sh`). Merging `main` auto-syncs to all apps. Self-merging your own PR is blocked by the auto-mode guard ŌĆö needs explicit user say-so ("you merge").

**Where a DB change goes** (per workspace `SUPABASE-MIGRATION.md`): shared/cross-app schema, views, RPCs, RLS, `supabase/migrations/*.sql` ŌåÆ shared-db (new `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_*.sql`, additive, preview ref `xjcyeuvzkhtzsheknaiu` before prod `qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai`). Service-specific additive columns on **app-owned** tables (e.g. PLM `GridViewState`, `GridLayout*`) stay in the backend as an idempotent `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` startup migration in `models/db.js` + model + `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js` ŌĆö these tables are NOT in shared-db's `core`/`plm` migration lineage, so record them as a note in `docs/app-migration-notes/`, not a SQL migration. The `forbid-shared-db-bypass.yml` CI (now in every app repo) fails PRs that add `supabase/migrations/*.sql` or reference DB secrets/`supabase db push` in scripts/workflows/package.json. See [[dflow-delivery-workflow]].

file.content:
### Column identity: never hardcode field names

```typescript
// Correct
params.data[this.getMasterFiledName('cbm_pc')]

// Wrong
params.data['CBM/pc']
```

### Duty rate: always use optional chaining

The duty rate can be stored as `{ title: "7.5" }` (object from autocomplete) or as a raw numeric string. Always:

```typescript
const dutyRateRaw = data[this.getMasterFiledName('duty_rate')];
const duty_rate = dutyRateRaw?.title ?? dutyRateRaw ?? 0;
```

### cellDataType: false on numeric string columns

input.old_string: ### Duty rate: always use optional chaining
input.new_string:
### Collapsible pricing column groups

The 28 pricing columns form 8 collapsible AG Grid column groups ŌĆö the cross of
{Gen, Lic} ├Ś {FOB, POE, Whse, mDDP}. "Price for Sales" stays visible when a group
is collapsed; the other columns (`columnGroupShow: 'open'`) show only when expanded.
Groups start expanded (`openByDefault: true`).

- **Grouping is a transform, not config.** `rfq.grid.config.ts` stays a **flat** array.
  `helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq-column-groups.ts` (`groupRfqPricingColumns`) wraps the
  pricing columns into `ColGroupDef`s as the **last** step when building `columnDefs`
  in `rfq.component.ts` ŌĆö *after* role filtering and auto-formatters run on the flat
  list. `colHeaders` stays flat (the layout dropdown and saved-view ordering depend on
  it); only the grid's `columnDefs` is nested. It groups by the `group-header-*`
  `headerClass` marker already on each pricing column.
- **Group open/closed state persists separately from column state.** AG Grid keeps it
  in its own structure (`getColumnGroupState()`/`setColumnGroupState()` ŌåÆ `[{groupId,
  open}]`), so it is NOT part of `getColumnState()`. It rides along in the existing Save
  View: captured in `saveRfqViewState()` (used by both "create view" and "save current
  view") and re-applied in `applyGridViewStateForViewName()`. Stored server-side in
  `GridViewState.column_group_state` (JSONB).

### Duty rate: always use optional chaining

content.content:
7	## File map
8	
9	| File | Role |
10	|---|---|
11	| `rfq.component.ts` | Master grid component. AG Grid setup, column wiring (valueSetters/valueGetters), dialog launches, row save/delete. ~4,100 lines. |
12	| `helpers/equation.ts` | All margin and sell-price calculations. Injectable service (`rfqEquation`). No AG Grid imports ŌĆö pure business math. |
13	| `helpers/api-error.ts` | HTTP error message extraction (shared across the app, lives here for historical reasons). |
14	| `helpers/price-sales-snapshot.ts` | Pure helpers (no AG Grid imports, unit-tested): `capturePriceSalesSnapshot()` snapshots duty rate + container price when a "Price for Sales" cell is edited; `buildSnapshotTooltip()` formats the tooltip text. |
15	| `price-sales-snapshot-renderer/` | Cell renderer for the 8 `*_price_sales` columns; renders the currency value and a native `title` hover tooltip built from the row's `price_sales_snapshots`. |
16	| `detail-cell-renderer/` | AG Grid master-detail renderer for vendor quotes. Each RFQ item expands to show its vendor rows. |
17	| `addNewProduct/` | Dialog for adding a new RFQ item (full form with all fields). |
18	| `addNewProductGrid/` | Alternative add-product flow using a mini-grid inside a dialog. |
19	| `duplicate_dialog/` | Duplicates an existing RFQ item (optionally copies vendors). |
20	| `container_cost_dialog/` | Dialog to set container cost (USD/container); triggers freight and landed-cost recalculation. |
21	| `duty_rate_dialog/` | Dialog to set the duty rate. |
22	| `multi_quote/` | Dialog to send quote requests to multiple vendors at once. |
23	| `autocomplete/` | Custom AG Grid cell editor for fields with server-loaded dropdown options. |
24	| `custom-dropdown/` | AG Grid cell editor for RFQ Group selection with add-new inline capability. |
25	| `numericEditor/` | AG Grid cell editor for numeric fields (validates > 0, < 1,000,000). |
26	| `datepicker/` | AG Grid cell editor for date fields. |
27	| `layout-dropdown/` | Toolbar component for saving/loading named grid view layouts. |
28	| `newVendor/` | Dialog to create a new vendor and assign it to an RFQ item. |
29	| `vendor/` | "Select Factories" dialog ŌĆö attach one or more factories to an RFQ item. Factories already on the item are shown red + unclickable so staff can't add a duplicate (see "Detail grid" below). |
30	
31	Grid column definitions live in **`src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts`**, not in this folder.
32

## File map

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `rfq.component.ts` | Master grid component. AG Grid setup, column wiring (valueSetters/valueGetters), dialog launches, row save/delete. ~4,100 lines. |

# AI Session Documentation Update Prompt

Use this at the end of an AI coding session when the session changed code, deployment behavior, data flow, configuration, operational assumptions, or important project knowledge.

Your job is not to rebuild the entire documentation system. 

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Update only the Markdown files that need durable knowledge from this specific session.

## Goal

Record what this session learned or changed so future developers and future AI sessions do not have to rediscover it.

Focus on:
- Information from this session that will still matter later.
- Idiosyncrasies that may look wrong but are intentional.
- Mistakes, dead ends, or assumptions from this session that future sessions should avoid.
- Architecture, data flow, deployment, configuration, or workflow changes.
- New operational steps, rollback notes, feature flags, identifiers, limits, or dependencies.
- Any temporary/incomplete work that needs a handoff.

## Rules

- Derive every doc update from code, config, migrations, workflows, commands run, or verified session findings.
- Do not document guesses as facts. Mark unknowns clearly and say how to verify them.
- Do not add secrets, tokens, passwords, private keys, or credential values.
- Do not duplicate large explanations across multiple docs. Put the short routing/context note in `AGENTS.md` only if future AI sessions need to see it early; put topic detail in the relevant doc under `docs/`.
- If the change does not require documentation updates, say so explicitly in the final report.

## What To Update

- `AGENTS.md`: only for high-signal guidance future AI sessions must see quickly, including new quirks, critical warnings, task routing, identifiers, or "do not repeat this mistake" notes.
- `HANDOFF.md`: create or update only if work is unfinished, blocked, partially deployed, or requires continuation context. Delete it only when the unfinished work it describes is truly complete.
- Topic docs under `docs/`: update the specific doc for the affected area, such as architecture, deployment, configuration, schema, worker logic, PopSG, Helper, Seafile, auth, bulk jobs, or known quirks.
- `README.md`: update only if the quick-start or top-level orientation changed.
- `CLAUDE.md`: update only for Claude Code-specific workflow rules. General AI/developer guidance belongs in `AGENTS.md`.

## Shared supabase.com Database / Backend Changes

If this session made any database, Supabase, migration, API contract, backend data-flow, auth/RLS, realtime, worker, or shared-backend change having to do with the shared supabase.com backend, also update the canonical shared database repo:

```text

u2giants/shared-db

https://github.com/u2giants/shared-db

```

This applies even when the code change was made inside an app repo. App repos may contain generated types, frontend adapters, workers, or local notes, but durable backend truth belongs in `shared-db`.

At minimum, document in `u2giants/shared-db`:

- What changed, including tables, columns, views, RPCs, triggers, functions, RLS policies, realtime publications, storage assumptions, worker/backend behavior, generated database types, or app-facing API contracts.

- Why it changed, including the product need, collision risk, bug, migration constraint, compatibility reason, or cross-app dependency.

- Which app or apps are affected: CRM, DAM, PM/PIM, Directus/PLM, workers, scripts, or shared admin tooling.

- Where the durable implementation lives: migration filenames, SQL files, scripts, API views/RPC names, worker files, generated type files, and relevant app repo commits if known.

- What was verified: local checks, `scripts/check-sql.sh`, Supabase dry-runs, preview branch pushes, app builds, smoke tests, RLS/role tests, data-parity checks, or reasons verification could not be completed.

- What remains risky or unfinished: production promotion status, data backfill needs, RLS gaps, app screens not migrated, deprecated contracts, rollback notes, or follow-up migrations.

Use the existing `shared-db` documentation structure:

- Put cross-app operating rules or urgent warnings in `AGENTS.md` only when future AI sessions must see them immediately.

- Put schema/API ownership, relationships, migration risks, or implementation notes in the matching doc under `docs/`.

- Put app migration handoffs under `docs/app-migration-notes/<app>-YYYYMMDD.md`.

- Put verification evidence under `docs/verification/` when it records a reusable database or preview-branch check.

- Add or update timestamped migrations under `supabase/migrations/`; never edit a migration that may already have been applied.

If a backend-relevant change was intentionally not documented in `shared-db`, state that explicitly in the final report and explain why.

## Required Session Note Shape

When adding session-derived knowledge, prefer concise entries like:

```md
### [Short decision or warning name]

What changed:
[One or two sentences.]

Why:
[Constraint, incident, bug, user decision, or operational reason.]

Future sessions should:
[Concrete instruction: what to do, what to avoid, where to verify.]
```

For unfinished work, include:

```md
## [Workstream name]

Status:
[done / partial / blocked / not started]

Done:
[Verified completed items.]

Next action:
[Exact next step.]

Risks / watchouts:
[Anything future sessions must not miss.]
```

## Verification Before Final Response

- Docs changed are limited to files that actually needed updates.
- New claims match the repository or verified session findings.
- No secrets were added.
- `HANDOFF.md` exists only if continuation context is needed.
- Any new quirk or warning explains why it exists and what would break if ignored.

## Final Report

Reply with:

```md
## Documentation Updates
| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `path/to/file.md` | Short summary |

## Handoff
- `HANDOFF.md`: present/absent
- Reason: ...

## Verification
- Docs verified against session findings: yes/no
- Secrets added: yes/no
- Unknowns remaining: ...
```

content.content:
=== shared-db: open PRs + note file + schema-map PLM row ===
--- schema-map line 79 ---
| PLM grid/view/admin | PLM `GridLayout`, `GridChildrenLayout`, `GridChildrenLayoutOrder`, `GridAccessLevel`, `GridViewState`, `GridCellNote`, `viewlayout`, `comments`, `user_notification`, `AuditLog`, `email_logs`, `AppSetting`, `AiCacheEvent` | `app.*` for generic concepts, `plm.*` for PLM-only | cache/generated/app-owned | Do not mix PLM grid layouts with PM saved views unless a shared view framework is intentionally designed. |

=== backend AGENTS.md ┬¦14 'Migrations live in db.js' (320-340) ===
**Do not change because:** The namespace name is internal. Renaming would touch every call-site for no user-facing benefit.

---

### Migrations live in `models/db.js`, not a migrations folder

**Looks like:** The wrong place for schema changes.

**Actually:** There is no `sequelize-cli`. The team uses inline `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` queries because they are idempotent and self-applying on every deploy.

**Pattern:**
```js
// Inside initialize() in models/db.js, after sequelize.sync():
sequelize.query(`ALTER TABLE ${schema}"TableName" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "col_name" TYPE`)
  .catch(err => console.warn('Migration <name> skipped:', err.message));
```

`${schema}` resolves to `"designflow".` in prod (or empty in test envs). Always add a matching assertion to `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`.

**Quote any mixed-case column identifier** (`"rfqItem_col"`), not just the table name. Sequelize quotes all identifiers, so an unquoted mixed-case column is folded to lowercase by Postgres, producing an orphan column Sequelize can never query (this caused SYS-002 ŌĆö see ┬¦14). Existing migrations already do this (e.g. `"rfqItem_duty_rate_equation"`, `"rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots"`). All-lowercase names (e.g. `factory_country`, `profile_photo`) don't need quoting, but quoting them anyway is harmless and consistent.

275:### Sample Tracking Customer and Carrier Quirks
326:## 11. Idiosyncratic decisions
--
376:### `window['itemService']` global references in rfq.component.ts
388:### `rfqEquation`, `prodEquation`, `licensingEquation` are separate injectable services
400:### AG Grid `colId` (stable) vs `field` (display/storage)
--
429:### `async valueSetter` with Promise rejection cancels the cell edit (AG Grid v35)
441:### RFQ fill handle keeps drag-copy but blocks double-click fill-down
--

input.new_string:
**Quote any mixed-case column identifier** (`"rfqItem_col"`), not just the table name. Sequelize quotes all identifiers, so an unquoted mixed-case column is folded to lowercase by Postgres, producing an orphan column Sequelize can never query (this caused SYS-002 ŌĆö see ┬¦14). Existing migrations already do this (e.g. `"rfqItem_duty_rate_equation"`, `"rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots"`). All-lowercase names (e.g. `factory_country`, `profile_photo`) don't need quoting, but quoting them anyway is harmless and consistent.

**This `db.js` inline pattern is only for app-owned tables (shared-db boundary ŌĆö 2026-07).** The database is now the shared Supabase project governed by `SUPABASE-MIGRATION.md` and the canonical `u2giants/shared-db` repo. Routing:
- **Additive column/table on an app-owned table** (`GridViewState`, `GridLayout*`, `RFQItem`, etc.) ŌåÆ keep it here: `models/db/*.js` model + idempotent `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` in `models/db.js` + `db.migration.test.js`. These tables are NOT in shared-db's `core`/`plm` migration lineage.

400	### AG Grid `colId` (stable) vs `field` (display/storage)
401	
402	**Looks like:** Redundant ŌĆö why have both `colId` and `field` on a column definition?
403	
404	**Actually:** In `rfq.grid.config.ts`, `field` is the human-readable storage key (e.g. `"CBM/pc"`, `"FOB Cost"`, `"Landed Cost (LDP port)"`). `colId` is the lowercase-underscore stable identifier (`"cbm_pc"`, `"fob_cost"`, `"landed_cost"`). All code that looks up a column by id uses `getMasterFiledName(colId)` to get the field name, then uses the field name to access `params.data[field]`.
405	
406	**Why:** AG Grid requires `field` for data binding; the codebase uses `colId` as the stable internal identifier for all programmatic access.
407	
408	**Do not change because:** Changing `field` names would break data read/write on every row. Changing `colId` values would break all `getMasterFiledName` lookups.
409	
410	---
411	
412	### Duty rate is stored twice: equation AND evaluated total
413	
414	**Looks like:** Redundant storage ŌĆö the cell has both an equation string (`"5.3+25+10"`) and an evaluated total (`40.3`).
415	
416	**Actually:** The grid cell displays the evaluated total (`40.3%`) so margin math in `equation.ts` can `parseFloat(duty_rate.title)`. The raw equation is stored separately so the user sees the breakdown when they click back into the cell (Excel-style).
417	
418	**Where it lives:**
419	- `[duty_rate field].title` ŌĆö the evaluated total as a string. Consumed by all downstream calculations in `equation.ts`.
420	- `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` ŌĆö the raw equation (DB column `VARCHAR(500)`). Persisted to the backend.
421	- `[duty_rate field]_equation` ŌĆö ephemeral mirror that `MathExprCellEditor` reads on edit. Forward-synced from `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` in the cell renderer; reverse-synced back in the column's `valueSetter`.
422	
423	**Why:** The duty rate is a sum of three components (MFN + Section 301 + Trump 2025) and that breakdown is auditable information. Storing only the evaluated total destroys it. Storing only the equation forces every downstream consumer to re-evaluate on every read.
424	
425	**Do not change because:** Removing `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` reverts users to opaque single-number duty rates ŌĆö they lose the ability to see what made up the 40.3% after the fact. Removing `.title` requires re-evaluating the equation in `equation.ts` on every margin calculation (and `equation.ts` runs hot ŌĆö 16 margin setters per row edit).
426	
427	---
428

file.content:
### AG Grid `colId` (stable) vs `field` (display/storage)

**Looks like:** Redundant ŌĆö why have both `colId` and `field` on a column definition?

**Actually:** In `rfq.grid.config.ts`, `field` is the human-readable storage key (e.g. `"CBM/pc"`, `"FOB Cost"`, `"Landed Cost (LDP port)"`). `colId` is the lowercase-underscore stable identifier (`"cbm_pc"`, `"fob_cost"`, `"landed_cost"`). All code that looks up a column by id uses `getMasterFiledName(colId)` to get the field name, then uses the field name to access `params.data[field]`.

**Why:** AG Grid requires `field` for data binding; the codebase uses `colId` as the stable internal identifier for all programmatic access.

**Do not change because:** Changing `field` names would break data read/write on every row. Changing `colId` values would break all `getMasterFiledName` lookups.

---

### Duty rate is stored twice: equation AND evaluated total

**Looks like:** Redundant storage ŌĆö the cell has both an equation string (`"5.3+25+10"`) and an evaluated total (`40.3`).

**Actually:** The grid cell displays the evaluated total (`40.3%`) so margin math in `equation.ts` can `parseFloat(duty_rate.title)`. The raw equation is stored separately so the user sees the breakdown when they click back into the cell (Excel-style).

**Where it lives:**
- `[duty_rate field].title` ŌĆö the evaluated total as a string. Consumed by all downstream calculations in `equation.ts`.
- `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` ŌĆö the raw equation (DB column `VARCHAR(500)`). Persisted to the backend.
- `[duty_rate field]_equation` ŌĆö ephemeral mirror that `MathExprCellEditor` reads on edit. Forward-synced from `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` in the cell renderer; reverse-synced back in the column's `valueSetter`.

**Why:** The duty rate is a sum of three components (MFN + Section 301 + Trump 2025) and that breakdown is auditable information. Storing only the evaluated total destroys it. Storing only the equation forces every downstream consumer to re-evaluate on every read.

**Do not change because:** Removing `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` reverts users to opaque single-number duty rates ŌĆö they lose the ability to see what made up the 40.3% after the fact. Removing `.title` requires re-evaluating the equation in `equation.ts` on every margin calculation (and `equation.ts` runs hot ŌĆö 16 margin setters per row edit).

---

input.old_string:
**Do not change because:** Changing `field` names would break data read/write on every row. Changing `colId` values would break all `getMasterFiledName` lookups.

---

### Duty rate is stored twice: equation AND evaluated total
input.new_string:
**Do not change because:** Changing `field` names would break data read/write on every row. Changing `colId` values would break all `getMasterFiledName` lookups.

---

### RFQ pricing column groups are a post-role-filter transform, not config (2026-07)

**Looks like:** The 28 pricing columns are grouped, so `rfq.grid.config.ts` should define nested `ColGroupDef`s.

**Actually:** `rfq.grid.config.ts` stays a **flat** array. `helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq-column-groups.ts` (`groupRfqPricingColumns`) wraps the pricing columns into 8 `ColGroupDef`s ({Gen,Lic}├Ś{FOB,POE,Whse,mDDP}) as the **last** step in `rfq.component.ts`, *after* `filterColumnsByRole` and `applyAutoFormatters` run on the flat list. `colHeaders` stays flat; only the grid's `columnDefs` is nested. Grouping keys off the `group-header-*` `headerClass` marker on each pricing column. "Price for Sales" is always visible; the rest are `columnGroupShow: 'open'`; groups `openByDefault: true`.

**Do not change because:** Nesting the source config directly would break `filterColumnsByRole`, `applyAutoFormatters`, and the layout dropdown ŌĆö they all iterate the flat list expecting each entry to have a `colId`. Column groups have `groupId`/`children` instead.

**Persistence quirk:** column-group open/closed state is NOT in `getColumnState()`. AG Grid keeps it separately (`getColumnGroupState()` ŌåÆ `[{groupId, open}]`). It rides along in the saved view via `saveRfqViewState()` ŌĆö the single helper both "create view" (`onSaveNewView`) and "save current view" (`onSaveLayout`) call. **Any new saved-view state MUST go through `saveRfqViewState()`**, or one save path silently drops it (that exact bug happened this session). Stored server-side in `GridViewState.column_group_state` (JSONB). See `src/app/pages/rfq/README.md` ŌåÆ "Collapsible pricing column groups".

---

### Duty rate is stored twice: equation AND evaluated total

Keep this column app-owned. If a future phase brings PLM Sequelize models under
canonical shared-db migrations, fold `GridViewState.column_group_state` into that
inventory rather than authoring a standalone `ALTER` migration against a table the
canonical `core`/`plm` schema does not otherwise manage.

Affected apps:
DesignFlow PLM only (frontend `designflow-frontend`, backend `designflow-backend`;
`designflow-bff` is a transparent pass-through ŌĆö no change). Not read by CRM, DAM,
PM/PIM, or Directus. The `saveGridViewState`/`getGridViewState` API contract gained
an optional `column_group_state` field, but that contract is PLM-internal
(frontend ŌåÆ bff ŌåÆ backend).

Where the durable implementation lives:
- Model: `designflow-backend/models/db/GridViewState.js` (`column_group_state` JSONB).
- Startup migration: `designflow-backend/models/db.js`
  (`ALTER TABLE "GridViewState" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS column_group_state JSONB`).
- Save/restore: `designflow-backend/models/lib.model.js`
  (`saveGridViewState`/`getGridViewState`); frontend `rfq.component.ts`
  (`saveRfqViewState`, `applyGridViewStateForViewName`) + `rfq-column-groups.ts`.
- Tests: `designflow-backend/tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`;
  `designflow-frontend/.../rfq-column-groups.spec.ts`, `rfq.component.spec.ts`.

What was verified:
App-side unit tests pass (backend `db.migration` 89/89; frontend transform 12/12,
`rfq.component` 58/58). NOT verified against a Supabase preview branch or with
`supabase db push --dry-run` ŌĆö this is an app-owned, additive, idempotent startup
migration that applies on the next `designflow-backend` deploy, not a canonical
`core`/`plm` migration, so the shared-db preview/promote gate does not apply.

Risks / watchouts:
Additive + nullable ŌåÆ no read/write break for any app (safe to deploy or roll back;
rollback is simply not writing the column). The column exists only once the updated
`designflow-backend` deploys and its startup migration runs. Not reflected in a
`supabase/migrations/*.sql` file by design; the canonical column-level record waits
on the PLM Sequelize inventory in `unified-supabase-migration-gaps.md`.

# DesignFlow PLM ŌĆö GridViewState.column_group_state ŌĆö 2026-07-02

## App-owned additive column (recorded for schema-map completeness)

What changed:
The DesignFlow PLM app added a `column_group_state` column (JSONB, nullable) to
the app-owned `GridViewState` table. It stores AG Grid column-group open/closed
state as `[{ groupId, open }]`, persisted per saved view alongside the existing
`column_state` and `filter_model` columns.

This is **not** a shared-schema change and there is **no** shared-db SQL migration
for it. `GridViewState` (like its sibling PLM grid/view tables `GridLayout`,
`GridChildrenLayout`, `GridChildrenLayoutOrder`, `GridAccessLevel`, `GridCellNote`,
`viewlayout`) is app-owned and managed by the PLM backend's own Sequelize layer,
not by the canonical `core`/`plm` migrations here. The column is created by:

- Model field: `designflow-backend/models/db/GridViewState.js`
- Idempotent startup migration: `designflow-backend/models/db.js`
  (`ALTER TABLE "GridViewState" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS column_group_state JSONB`)
- Regression assertions: `designflow-backend/tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`

Why:
The RFQ grid now groups its 28 pricing columns into 8 collapsible AG Grid column
groups (Gen/Lic ├Ś FOB/POE/Whse/mDDP). AG Grid keeps column-group open/closed state
in a structure separate from column state, so persisting each user's collapse
choice in their saved view required this additive column. Per the workspace
`SUPABASE-MIGRATION.md` Decision Principle, changes to existing DesignFlow
functionality on an app-owned table stay in the backend service as an idempotent,
additive, non-destructive startup migration ŌĆö which this is.

Why it's recorded here:
`docs/unified-supabase-migration-gaps.md` lists "Generate column-level inventories
for PopDAM tables and PLM Sequelize models" as pending. Until that inventory
exists, app-owned column additions like this one are noted here so the canonical
schema record stays complete. See the PLM grid/view/admin row in
`docs/unified-supabase-schema-map.md`.

Future sessions should:
Keep this column app-owned. If a future phase brings PLM Sequelize models under
canonical shared-db migrations, fold `GridViewState.column_group_state` into that
inventory rather than authoring a standalone `ALTER` migration against a table the
canonical `core`/`plm` schema does not otherwise manage.

inventory rather than authoring a standalone `ALTER` migration against a table the
canonical `core`/`plm` schema does not otherwise manage.
+`designflow-bff` is a transparent pass-through ŌĆö no change). Not read by CRM, DAM,

+`supabase db push --dry-run` ŌĆö this is an app-owned, additive, idempotent startup
+migration that applies on the next `designflow-backend` deploy, not a canonical
+`core`/`plm` migration, so the shared-db preview/promote gate does not apply.
+Additive + nullable ŌåÆ no read/write break for any app (safe to deploy or roll back;
+rollback is simply not writing the column). The column exists only once the updated
+`designflow-backend` deploys and its startup migration runs. Not reflected in a
+`supabase/migrations/*.sql` file by design; the canonical column-level record waits

All merged, clean, and pushed. Here's the report in the format your prompt asked for.

## Documentation Updates

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `designflow-backend/AGENTS.md` | Added a `GridViewState` row to the ┬¦8 data-model table (incl. `column_group_state`); added a ┬¦14 note drawing the **app-owned `db.js` migration vs. `shared-db`** boundary and citing the new `forbid-shared-db-bypass.yml` CI ŌĆö so a future session won't wrongly drop a `supabase/migrations/*.sql` into the app repo. |
| `designflow-frontend/AGENTS.md` | New ┬¦11 quirk: RFQ pricing groups are a **post-role-filter transform** (not nested config), why nesting config would break role-filtering, and the **"all saved-view state must go through `saveRfqViewState()`"** rule (records the one-path-drift bug from this session). |
| `designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/rfq/README.md` | New "Collapsible pricing column groups" section + file-map entry for `rfq-column-groups.ts`. |
| `u2giants/shared-db` `docs/app-migration-notes/designflow-plm-gridviewstate-column-group-state-20260702.md` | Expanded with **Affected apps / Where implemented / Verified / Risks** sections. Merged to `main` (PR #35 ŌåÆ `1494b29`). |
| `u2giants/shared-db` `docs/unified-supabase-schema-map.md` | Added a pointer from the PLM grid/view row to the column + note. Merged to `main`. |

Not changed (deliberately): `HANDOFF.md` (no unfinished work), `CLAUDE.md` (no Claude-workflow rule change), top-level `README.md` (no orientation change), and **no `supabase/migrations/*.sql`** ŌĆö the column is app-owned and that lane is CI-forbidden in app repos.

## Handoff
- `HANDOFF.md`: **absent** (not created).
- Reason: the feature is code-complete and unit-tested; the DB column is additive/idempotent and applies on the normal `designflow-backend` deploy. The only "open" items are app PRs #141/#58 to `develop`, which are your standard review flow, not blocked work.

## Verification
- Docs verified against session findings: **yes** ŌĆö every claim traces to code I wrote/read (`rfq-column-groups.ts`, `saveRfqViewState`, `GridViewState.js`, `db.js` migration, `forbid-shared-db-bypass.yml`) or tests I ran (backend `db.migration` 89/89; frontend transform 12/12, `rfq.component` 58/58).
- Secrets added: **no**.
- Unknowns remaining: the migration was **not** validated against a Supabase preview branch or `supabase db push --dry-run` ŌĆö by design, since it's an app-owned startup migration, not a canonical `core`/`plm` migration. To confirm in production: after the next `designflow-backend` deploy, check that `GridViewState.column_group_state` exists (or that saving/reloading a view with a collapsed group persists it).

Everything you asked for is landed: shared-db merged, and a comprehensive, session-scoped `.md` pass across all four repos.
